C[matburt: I'm about to head to bed but I thought I'd make a move real quick and I'll pick it up tomorrow
matburt: Welcome to Nova, by the way!
pete: hey... I'm the creator of goshrine.com... awesome site!
pete: Who are you guys?  I really like what you've done.
C[matburt: Thanks! We're just 2 guys with a passion for writing code and wanted to put together an awesome Go site.
C[pete: well, what I've seen so far has blown me away.  you guys put some serious time into this.
C[pete: you guys are much closer than kaya to implementing what I thought would be the ideal go site.
pete: If I had more time to work on goshrine, I would be adding a lot of the things you've already added (game overviews, notifications, friending, etc)
pete: but I'll let you catch some sleep
matburt: Thanks for the kind words! We're really proud of what we have done so far.
C[pete: Is it a python back end?
matburt: django drives the core site while node drives the game itself... and the chat
C[pete: conditional moves is very cool
column2: I don't think I've seen that on a server yet.

column1: matburt: Yeah... that's one thing both anoek and I really like from playing chess online so we thought we'd implement it here
C[pete: so ""no va"" in spanish means ""doesn't go"" :)
matburt: that's interesting... you know, I had not thought of that
matburt: side effect of being English guys I guess :)
C[matburt: Sorry it's taking me a little while to move sometimes :)
C[pete: no prob.  This is my first correspondance game, actually.  Kind of enjoying it.
matburt: I tend to prefer them... between code and family I don't have a lot of time to play a game all in one sitting.
C[pete: yeah, I hear you. I have a 6 yr old and a 3yr old, and help run a software shop.  Doesn't leave a lot of time.
matburt: I have an 8 month old... things have started to settle down a little bit
C[pete: unlike go, which just keeps getting harder.
C[matburt: Just pushed up a new version of the site
C[pete: cool, what changes did you make?
pete: I just pushed a new update to manymo.com as well. :)
matburt: posted the info up on the news: http://nova.gs/news
matburt: hey I think I've heard of manymo
matburt: So part of my job is build Android (and IOS) applications... and I have quite a few android app in the market.
matburt: Was looking at an easier way to test on a larger scale
pete: turn notification on the games page is great.
pete: heh, wow, we share a lot in common. (you're a mobile dev as well)
matburt: yep, and the icon up there at the top listing the number of games that it's your turn in and will take you right to a game
matburt: can't wait to get started on Nova's mobile apps
C[pete: I just love this analyze game feature.  I've used it probably hundreds of times this game.
C[matburt: Apparently that's a very controversial feature in the Go community
matburt: much to our surprise... that's another one we really loved coming from playing a lot of chess
pete: hmm, that seems odd.  I did notice that you have the ability to disable it for some games.
matburt: Yeah... some folks were really adamant about being able to turn it off
C[pete: phooey.  it's your server. do what you want.  A lot of users are wrong.
C[pete: add too many features to support all the little niggling details people ask for, and you'll have a nightmare of a server.
matburt: I tend to agree, but we are hoping to be as successful as possible and sometimes that means choosing our battles
column2: )   It's easy enough to add a little knob in the game creation screen.

column1: C[pete: Would be curious to know if there was anything missing from manymo that you guys would need for testing.

matburt: I think at the time I was looking at it we were just really early in the development process. I see that I can fire up and test on it so tomorrow morning I'll load up some apps and let you know
C[pete: were you looking for interactive testing, or automated testing?
pete: was just thinking it's getting late for me, but you're eastern, so you're even up later.
matburt: I don't get much sleep these days
matburt: a lot of our testing is somewhat manual because of the need to link to a remote service
matburt: we're also pretty location heavy
pete: as in feeding the emulator gps coords?
matburt: here's an example of what my company does: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.velleros.notificationclient.westcentral
pete: I think you should be able to do that with the adb connection.
matburt: and I have a pretty stout system so I can run a bunch simltaneously
matburt: but it's useful to find better ways to get good test coverage
pete: we don't support starting emulators from the command line yet
column2: that will likely be a paying feature.

column1: matburt: Well, I'd like to check it out even if it isn't an exact fit for what I do
matburt: it's a very interesting solution
C[matburt: Here's another one of my apps... this one is open source
pete: we're still trying to figure out where we want to go with it
pete: emacs is living on into the mobile world.

C[matburt: Yeah, I don't see it going away anytime soon.

column2: I was pretty solidly into emacs, writing lots of elisp, and now I'm back to vi.
